text: Microsoft states that it is pursuing carbon negative, water positive, and zero waste goals by 2030, with environmental reporting focused on carbon, water, waste, and ecosystems.
Microsoft reported progress toward its environmental goals but also acknowledged pressure from Scope 3 and supplier-related emissions linked to business growth and infrastructure demand.
Apple describes its Apple 2030 plan as a strategy to reduce greenhouse gas emissions through recycled materials, renewable energy, lower-carbon transport, and carbon removal.
Apple's environmental reporting highlights increased use of recycled and renewable materials across products to reduce product lifecycle emissions.
Walmart says its sustainability work includes reducing greenhouse gas emissions, regenerating natural resources, reducing product and packaging waste, and improving supply chain resilience.
Walmart reported that nearly half of its global electricity needs were supplied by renewable sources in 2024, while continuing to track progress against long-term climate goals.
Amazon reported that all electricity consumed by its operations was matched with renewable energy sources in 2024 for the second consecutive year.
Amazon's 2024 sustainability report discusses decarbonization, waste prevention, responsible diversion of waste, and broader sustainability initiatives across business units.
Coca-Cola was criticized by campaigners after changing a reusable packaging target and emphasizing recycled content and collection goals instead of the earlier reuse commitment.
Los Angeles County sued PepsiCo and Coca-Cola over allegations that the companies misled the public about plastic bottle recyclability and environmental impacts.
A German court ruled that Apple could not advertise its Apple Watch as carbon neutral in Germany, citing concerns about carbon offset claims and long-term project certainty.
The SEC adopted rules to enhance and standardize climate-related disclosures by public companies, reflecting investor demand for comparable climate risk information.
Nike's sustainability materials frame climate change as a major challenge and describe innovation efforts intended to reduce environmental impact across products and operations.
Apple reported continued work on supplier clean energy, recycled inputs, and lower-carbon product design as part of its broader emissions reduction plan.
Nike reported maintaining pay equity for women globally and U.S. racial and ethnic minorities, while continuing efforts to diversify leadership and strengthen representation.
Nike's sustainability data discusses injury rates and worker safety metrics covering full-time and certain temporary workers, indicating ongoing tracking of workplace safety performance.
Starbucks states that responsible sourcing and human rights practices are embedded in coffee and environmental supply chain initiatives involving farms and suppliers.
Brazilian workers filed a lawsuit alleging forced-labor-like conditions on coffee farms connected to Starbucks supply chains, raising serious supply chain human rights concerns.
Coca-Cola HBC states that it is committed to identifying, preventing, and mitigating adverse human rights impacts connected with employees and business relationships.
Unilever's sustainability statement describes board-level oversight of human rights due diligence and risk management through governance bodies and committees.
Walmart says its sustainability priorities include supporting people in supply chains through responsible sourcing and inclusive economic opportunity.
Amazon's sustainability reporting references workplace safety programs and supply chain standards as part of its broader sustainability operating model.
Starbucks works with coffee farms and suppliers to deliver products while also supporting economic development in the communities tied to its sourcing network.
Coca-Cola HBC describes employee onboarding and leadership programs that cover diversity, equity, inclusion, and human rights topics.
Unilever states that human rights remain a core part of its sustainability agenda and are connected to how the company manages social impact across operations.
Amazon's report highlights efforts to build a more sustainable company for partners, employees, and communities while continuing to manage workplace and supply chain priorities.
The SEC charged WisdomTree with failing to follow its own investment criteria for ESG-marketed funds and with misstating exposure to fossil fuels and tobacco.
The SEC charged Deutsche Bank subsidiary DWS over misstatements regarding ESG investments and related compliance failures, leading to a monetary settlement.
German prosecutors fined DWS after finding that the asset manager made misleading ESG-related statements in marketing and documentation.
The SEC charged Vale with misleading investors about dam safety and related environmental and economic risks before a major mining disaster.
The Apple Watch carbon-neutral ruling raised governance concerns about the substantiation and oversight of environmental marketing claims.
The SEC climate disclosure rule increased governance expectations for board oversight, risk controls, and reliable public-company climate reporting.
Unilever states that its board and corporate responsibility committee oversee human rights due diligence and risk management, linking social risk to governance oversight.
Walmart frames ESG reporting as part of building stakeholder trust while managing emissions, responsible sourcing, natural resources, and supply chain resilience.
Microsoft's sustainability reporting describes transparency and accountability around environmental goals, which supports governance oversight of climate commitments.
Starbucks identifies supply chain sustainability and climate-related risks in its annual reporting, indicating governance attention to operational and sourcing risks.
The Los Angeles County lawsuit against beverage companies alleges misleading environmental claims, creating governance risk around disclosure controls and marketing oversight.
The WisdomTree case shows that ESG fund governance requires internal controls capable of ensuring marketed investment screens are actually implemented.
Starbucks' annual report discusses risks related to opening new stores, scaling supply chain infrastructure, and adapting product offerings to consumer preferences.
Feng Tay reported that despite a turbulent global economy and sportswear industry competition, revenue increased slightly and profit metrics outperformed the previous year.
Coca-Cola FEMSA described 2024 as part of a broader transformation while reporting business progress and implementation of strategic initiatives.
Amazon's sustainability report covers multiple business units including AWS, Devices, Amazon Fresh, Whole Foods Market, Stores, and Twitch.
Apple announced new product design and materials information across its devices as part of broader product development and operations updates.
Microsoft provides annual sustainability updates alongside broader corporate responsibility reporting for customers, partners, and employees.
Nike publishes sustainability data files as part of its corporate reporting package and uses them to provide structured information to stakeholders.
Walmart describes sustainability as connected to everyday low cost, supply certainty, innovation, and long-term business resilience.
Starbucks annual reporting discusses market factors, supply chain infrastructure, product innovation, and customer response as business risks.
Unilever's annual reporting covers company performance, strategic priorities, and operating context in addition to sustainability disclosures.
Coca-Cola HBC describes leadership programs and internal policy communication as part of general management and employee onboarding processes.
Amazon's report explains that sustainability coverage spans subsidiaries and business units, which is a reporting scope statement rather than a specific ESG risk event.
